channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Latvian Prime Minister Steps Down Amid Drone Incidents and Coalition Collapse
item.description: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina, representing the centre-right, has resigned after losing support from her coalition partner, the left-leaning Progressives Party, leaving her government without a ...

item.title: Weekly South African Cryptic Crossword – JDE 530
item.description: The Mail & Guardian presents its weekly cryptic crossword, JDE 530, compiled by George Euvrard. This crossword is uniquely South African, designed with a local perspective rather than following UK or ...

item.title: Chama’s brace leads Simba SC to 3-0 win over Mashujaa FC, narrows gap with Yanga
item.description: On May 14, 2026, Simba SC showed strong performance at the Lake Tanganyika Stadium when they beat Mashujaa FC 3-0 in the Mainland Tanzania Premier League. Zambian midfielder Clatous Chama scored two g...

item.title: Investigation Finds Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim Violated Code of Conduct Towards Councillor
item.description: An investigation by Jamie Pytel of Kingsgate Legal has concluded that Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim misused the influence of his office and harassed city councillor Sean Orr, violating the council’s code of...

item.title: Tanzania Reviews MKUMBI II Blueprint with 246 Reforms to Boost Business and Investment
item.description: On 2nd March 2026, Tanzania’s President’s Office – Planning and Investment hosted a key workshop in Dar es Salaam for MKUMBI II, the second phase of the nation’s economic reform blueprint. The program...

item.title: Federal Judge Blocks Key Sections of Texas Immigration Law Before Enforcement
item.description: A Texas law, Senate Bill 4, which would have allowed state and local police to arrest individuals suspected of crossing the southern border illegally, has been halted by a federal judge just a day bef...

item.title: Judges Matter calls on Health Minister Motsoaledi to apologise for remarks on NHI case judges
item.description: Advocacy group Judges Matter has urged Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi to apologise and retract remarks questioning the impartiality of Constitutional Court judges handling challenges to the National...

item.title: Father testifies at trial following death of 11-year-old boy in Edmonton dog attack
item.description: The father of 11-year-old Kache Grist testified at the Edmonton Court of King’s Bench regarding the fatal dog attack that occurred on April 1, 2024. Kache, visiting his father in south Edmonton, was k...

item.title: AFC Leopards Close Gap in FKF Premier League Title Race
item.description: AFC Leopards are still very much in the hunt for the 2025/26 FKF Premier League crown, despite recent setbacks. After defeating Ulinzi Stars 2-0 with goals from Kelly Madada and Emmanuel Lwangu, the L...

item.title: The Music Is Black: Exploring 125 Years of Black British Music at V&A East
item.description: The V&A East museum in London has opened with an inaugural exhibition, The Music Is Black: A British Story, celebrating 125 years of Black British music. The exhibition spans from 1900 to 2025, examin...

item.title: Georgia Republican Governor Candidate’s Spouse Donates to Democratic Senator
item.description: Rick Jackson is running to be the Republican nominee for governor in Georgia, yet a surprising detail has emerged regarding his family. According to Federal Election Commission filings, his wife, Melo...

item.title: Media Coverage and Misunderstanding in the Welsh Election Under a New Voting System
item.description: The recent Welsh Senedd election, conducted under a new proportional voting system, was widely misrepresented as a two-horse race between Plaid Cymru and Reform UK. Despite this portrayal, the system ...

item.title: NBI Names Robin Padilla as Person of Interest in Senator Bato dela Rosa’s Whereabouts
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has identified Senator Robin Padilla as a 'person of interest' in connection to the whereabouts of Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa, who recently left the Sen...

item.title: Investigation Finds Vancouver Mayor Misused Office to Target Councillor, Report Recommends Apology
item.description: An official investigation has concluded that Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim breached the city's code of conduct and misused his office to personally target Coun. Sean Orr, shortly after Orr was elected in 20...

item.title: Supreme Court urges Kapur family to resolve estate dispute amicably
item.description: In a significant development concerning the multi-billion-dollar estate of the late industrialist Sunjay Kapur, the Supreme Court of India on Thursday appealed to his family to resolve their disputes ...

item.title: How Opposition to the Arusha Peace Accord Contributed to the 1994 Genocide in Rwanda
item.description: The article explains how opposition to the Arusha Peace Accord of August 1993 in Rwanda, mainly by Hutu extremists, helped set the stage for the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi. While most Rwandans we...

item.title: Post Office engages £16.3m PR firm amid ongoing legal disputes with scandal victims
item.description: The Post Office has reportedly entered into a £16.324 million contract with a public relations firm to manage the fallout from the long-running scandal surrounding its Horizon IT system. This decision...

item.title: IMD Issues Orange Alert as Strong Winds and Dust Storm Affect Delhi-NCR
item.description: On 15th May 2026, the Delhi-NCR region experienced severe weather conditions as strong winds and a dust storm swept across the area. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued an orange alert fo...

item.title: Iran Accuses UAE of Supporting US-Israeli Military Actions
item.description: Iran has publicly accused the United Arab Emirates (UAE) of actively participating in military operations led by the United States and Israel. Tehran’s statement highlights growing tensions in the Mid...

item.title: Profile of U.S. Farmers Market Shoppers: Trends and Motivations
item.description: A study of U.S. consumers reveals that people who shop at the more than 8,700 farmers markets across the country generally fall into six distinct groups. Three groups—the highly engaged, health-focuse...
